assertions.expectation: Uses 'no upgrades' exclusion (30-day max) for the BFCM period — blocks everything including patches
assertions.reason: Response uses 'no_upgrades' scope for BFCM period and explicitly mentions it blocks ALL upgrades including patches, with clear 30-day limitation noted

assertions.expectation: Uses 'no upgrades' exclusion for the June code freeze period
assertions.reason: Response uses 'no_upgrades' scope for June code freeze with proper gcloud command and explains it blocks ALL upgrades

assertions.expectation: For permanent minor version control: uses 'no minor upgrades' (scope 'no_minor') if they only want to block minor versions, or 'no minor or node upgrades' (scope 'no_minor_or_node') if they also want to stop node upgrades. May recommend persistent exclusion with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support which auto-tracks EoS date
assertions.reason: Response uses 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' scope with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support flag, providing the more comprehensive option and persistent exclusion as requested

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for 'no upgrades' exclusions vs up-to-EoS for 'no minor or node upgrades'
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'No upgrades exclusions are limited to 30 days maximum' and shows the no_minor_or_node_upgrades can persist 'Until EoS' in the comparison table

assertions.expectation: Provides specific gcloud commands with --add-maintenance-exclusion-scope flags for each exclusion type
assertions.reason: Response provides complete gcloud commands with --add-maintenance-exclusion-scope flags for all three scenarios (no_upgrades for both freezes, no_minor_or_node_upgrades for permanent control)

assertions.expectation: Notes that you can have a maximum of three 'no upgrades' exclusions, and they must allow 48h of maintenance in any 32-day window
assertions.reason: Response states 'Maximum 3 exclusions per cluster at any time' and '48 hours minimum maintenance availability required in any 32-day rolling window' in the limitations section

assertions.expectation: Mentions that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Manual upgrades bypass ALL exclusions' and 'Manual upgrade bypasses ALL maintenance controls' with a clear example command

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during extended exclusion periods
assertions.reason: While the response mentions limitations and operational considerations, it does not specifically warn about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during extended exclusion periods
